prompts.text: "Primary colours" — red, yellow and blue. We call them primary because we can't make them by mixing; they're the starting colours.
"Secondary colours" — orange, green and purple. Each one is made by mixing two primary colours together.
Before mixing, ask: "I wonder what red and yellow will make?" — let them predict before touching the slots.
After a mix, say together: "[colour A] and [colour B] made [result]!" — then say it once more slowly.
Point to a colour on the wheel and ask: "Is this one closer to red or yellow?" — builds the idea that colours are related.
